headline: Cabinet committees bypass parliament on 23 policy decisions
context: Twenty-three major policy decisions in 2025 were made through cabinet committees rather than parliamentary debate, including changes affecting taxation, education, and environmental standards.

cards.big: 23 major policies decided by cabinet committee — Parliament never debated any of them
cards.sub: Executive increasingly governs through committee rather than through legislature

cards.big: Cabinet committee decisions have force of law through administrative orders
cards.sub: Executive instruments bypass legislative scrutiny entirely while having equivalent effect

cards.big: Policy changes affected estimated RM45 billion in government spending allocations
cards.sub: Massive fiscal decisions made without the transparency of parliamentary budget process

cards.big: Number of cabinet committees grew from 5 in 2020 to 14 in 2025
cards.sub: Proliferation of committees signals executive preference for governing without Parliament

cards.big: Every decision made by cabinet committee instead of Parliament is a decision made without the people's representatives
Should major policy changes above a fiscal threshold require parliamentary debate and approval?
